Name: spectral_latency_aware_inference
Title: Spectral Latency-Aware Inference: Training-Free Test-Time Optimization via Norm-Preserving Subspace Projection
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that restricting test-time optimization to a low-rank, norm-preserving subspace can ensure strict computational budgets while preserving the optimization landscape's geometry, leading to efficient and latency-aware inference.
Related Work: Our proposal builds upon recent works such as Spectral Quantization Routing, Training-Free Geometric Interference Cancellation, and Geometric Signal Separation, which have shown the potential of spectral methods for efficient and robust inference. However, our approach differs in its focus on test-time optimization and latency-awareness, and its use of norm-preserving subspace projection to ensure strict computational budgets.
Abstract: We propose Spectral Latency-Aware Inference, a training-free framework that restricts test-time optimization to a low-rank, norm-preserving subspace. Our approach ensures strict computational budgets while preserving the optimization landscape's geometry, leading to efficient and latency-aware inference. We theoretically ground our method in information-theoretic bounds and demonstrate its effectiveness on mathematical reasoning and code generation tasks, reducing hidden-state perturbation overhead by over 70% and enforcing deterministic latency bounds.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed method on mathematical reasoning and code generation tasks, comparing its performance to full-space perturbation baselines and other state-of-the-art methods.
Assess the effectiveness of the norm-preserving subspace projection in reducing hidden-state perturbation overhead and enforcing deterministic latency bounds.
Investigate the impact of the proposed method on the optimization landscape's geometry and the resulting inference efficiency.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed method may not be effective for all types of tasks or models, and may require careful tuning of hyperparameters.
The use of norm-preserving subspace projection may introduce additional computational overhead, which could impact the overall efficiency of the method.
